title: Fix create_test for remote integration
body:
fixes tests to pass for remote integration.  the two remaining tests that are skipped are due to lack of logs command.

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
LGTM
one issue with this is that we are no longer testing the -l flag, but I don't think that is a big deal.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Can we ensure that we do not completely drop testing of `--latest` as part of this? Adding separate, gated tests that only run local seems like a good idea.
